payload.summary: The paid DeepSeek V4 Pro path passes a live child-agent smoke test, but FishToucher cannot yet validate a durable receipt that binds a packet to the actual provider request and response.
claims: The current message envelope records a declared provider and model only.
The verified runtime chain is GPT parent to Codex coordinator to the Keychain-backed DeepSeek V4 Pro adapter.
A self-declared provider label is not sufficient invocation provenance.
requests: Add a dependency-free invocation receipt validator.
Expose receipt validation through the FishToucher CLI.
Cover malformed hashes, timestamps, and exit-status contradictions with regression tests.

payload.summary: Add a model-neutral v1 invocation receipt validator and CLI command so real provider calls can be hash-bound to an authorized packet.
acceptance: A dependency-free validator accepts a fishtoucher.invocation/v1 object with exact top-level fields for invocation, run, packet, role, provider, model, transport, coordinator identity, request and response SHA-256 values, UTC start/end timestamps, status, and exit code.
The validator rejects unknown fields, blank identities, malformed SHA-256 values or timestamps, end-before-start ordering, and contradictions between success/failure status and exit code.
The receipt stores hashes and routing identity only; prompts, responses, credentials, and machine-specific secret locations are excluded.
The CLI validates one invocation receipt through a new invocation command.
Focused tests cover a valid DeepSeek receipt and adversarial structural and semantic failures.
requests: Acknowledge the packet before applying the DeepSeek-proposed patch.
Return exact changed files, commands, raw results, unresolved risks, and a skill-evolve decision.
payload.provider_note: This packet must be authored by the paid DeepSeek V4 Pro API through the registered Codex coordinator and secure adapter. The coordinator may gather context, apply DeepSeek's proposed patch, and run commands, but must not replace DeepSeek's implementation reasoning.

payload.summary: Accepted the bounded invocation-receipt packet. A Codex gpt-5.3-codex-spark coordinator transported the paid DeepSeek V4 Pro-authored response and is responsible only for applying that response mechanically and running the authorized verification commands.
claims: The secure DeepSeek adapter exited with status 0.
The implementation author is DeepSeek V4 Pro; the Codex coordinator does not replace its design reasoning.
requests: Have the GPT sensor independently inspect the resulting diff and allocate the next mailbox sequence.

payload.summary: DeepSeek V4 Pro supplied a dependency-free invocation receipt validator, CLI route, and focused tests; all authored checks passed.
claims: The implementation remained inside the three-file write scope.
No prompt, response, credential, or secret location was committed.
The Codex coordinator changed only invalid patch framing before applying DeepSeek's implementation.
requests: Independently probe protocol tagging, hash validation, timestamp ordering, and exit-status consistency.

payload.summary: Reject: the serialized receipt is not self-identifying because the validator omits and rejects the required fishtoucher.invocation/v1 protocol marker.
claims: An untagged receipt passes validation.
The correct fishtoucher.invocation/v1 marker and unrelated protocol markers are both rejected as unknown fields.
Green authored tests do not satisfy the packet's serialized protocol-version contract.
requests: Require protocol with the exact value fishtoucher.invocation/v1.
Add missing, wrong, and valid protocol regression tests, including CLI behavior.
Rerun every packet and repository gate.
payload.proof_boundary: Matching hashes bind the ACK to local request and response byte streams but do not independently attest remote provider identity or coordinator fidelity.

payload.summary: The first packet is closed as rejected; the changed hypothesis is that protocol identity is serialized data, not module-level documentation.
claims: The initial packet exhausted its single implementation attempt.
The repair requires no scope or interface expansion beyond enforcing the named v1 protocol.
requests: Execute the separately bounded repair packet without rewriting the failed attempt.

payload.summary: Repair the rejected receipt contract by making fishtoucher.invocation/v1 a required serialized protocol marker.
payload.base_revision: 9e38d87 plus the rejected packet-001 candidate diff
acceptance: Define the exact protocol constant fishtoucher.invocation/v1.
Require protocol as an exact top-level receipt field and reject any other value.
Add focused validator tests for valid, missing, and wrong protocol markers.
Add a CLI regression proving the wrong protocol fails through the invocation command.
Preserve every previously accepted structural and semantic check.
claims: The initial failed attempt remains preserved as mailbox sequences 3 through 6.
requests: Return exact verification output and fresh request/response hashes.
payload.provider_note: The repair must again be authored by paid DeepSeek V4 Pro through the registered secure adapter; the Codex coordinator may transport and apply the response mechanically.

payload.summary: Accepted repair packet-002. Paid DeepSeek V4 Pro authored the repair response; a Codex gpt-5.3-codex-spark coordinator transported it and will only apply its design mechanically and run the authorized gates.
claims: The secure DeepSeek request was dispatched at 2026-07-15T11:17:03Z; its exact response was persisted by 2026-07-15T11:19:05Z; the adapter exited with status 0.
Mailbox sequences 1 through 7 remain immutable.
requests: Have the GPT sensor allocate sequence 9 after independently inspecting the repair.

payload.summary: The DeepSeek-authored repair makes the v1 protocol marker required and exact-matched, with validator and CLI regressions.
claims: Protocol identity is now serialized, required, and exact-matched.
The repair stayed inside its two-file implementation scope.
The coordinator only normalized invalid patch context and import ordering; it did not change DeepSeek's implementation logic.
requests: Independently rerun the protocol probes and all repository gates.
payload.skill_evolve: update: serialized protocol markers must be required, exact-matched, and tested at validator and CLI boundaries

payload.summary: Accept: the real DeepSeek repair satisfies the bounded invocation-receipt contract and all independent gates.
evidence_checked: correct, missing, and wrong protocol validator and CLI probes
flow, evidence, message, mailbox, and invocation validators
two-file repair scope and protected paths
four mode-600 runtime request and response artifact hashes
claims: Both paid DeepSeek calls are represented by structurally valid, hash-bound receipts.
The rejected first result remains preserved in mailbox history.
The accepted repair is independently verified and does not modify protected contracts.
requests: Close the iteration while retaining unsigned-receipt and append-only-storage risks.
payload.proof_boundary: Local hashes bind receipts to byte streams but do not cryptographically attest the remote provider, adapter exit status, or coordinator fidelity.
payload.skill_evolve: update: serialized protocol markers must be required, exact-matched, and tested at validator and CLI boundaries

payload.summary: Software-loop-002 is closed with a real paid DeepSeek executor lane, an independently rejected first attempt, a repaired and accepted result, and two validated invocation receipts.
claims: The executor provider was DeepSeek V4 Pro; the local Codex coordinator used gpt-5.3-codex-spark only for context transport, patch application, and command execution.
A separate GPT verifier rejected the unversioned first result and accepted the protocol-tagged repair.
All repository gates are green at closeout.
requests: Add signed provider attestations before treating receipts as cryptographic identity proof.
Add coordinator-owned append storage before claiming historical mailbox immutability.
payload.skill_evolve: updated in FishToucher AGENTS.md and normative documentation
